clinicalProblem.clinicalIntent: Classify hypertension control status in patients already taking antihypertensive medication based on office and out-of-office BP measurements
clinicalProblem.whenToUse: Follow-up visits for patients on antihypertensive therapy, medication adjustment decisions, treatment response assessment
clinicalProblem.decisionOrAction: Hypertension classification (controlled/uncontrolled/masked uncontrolled/white-coat effect) + treatment adjustment recommendation

navigationPath.purpose: Entry point for patients on antihypertensive medication
Determine if office BP is high (≥130/80 or threshold per guidelines)
Determine if out-of-office BP is high (≥130/80 or threshold per guidelines)
Apply 2x2 matrix logic to determine category
Display final category with clinical implications
Recommend next steps based on category

rules: Determine if office BP is high using guideline threshold (typically ≥130/80 or ≥140/90 per guidelines)
Determine if out-of-office BP is high using guideline threshold (typically ≥130/80 or ≥135/85 for home monitoring)
- Office High? No + Out-of-office High? No → Controlled hypertension
- Office High? Yes + Out-of-office High? Yes → Uncontrolled hypertension
- Office High? No + Out-of-office High? Yes → Masked uncontrolled hypertension
- Office High? Yes + Out-of-office High? No → White-coat effect
Flag medication adjustment needs based on category

categories.name: Uncontrolled hypertension
categories.clinical_meaning: True inadequate BP control despite medication

categories.name: Masked uncontrolled hypertension
categories.clinical_meaning: BP appears controlled in clinic but not at home → increased cardiovascular risk

categories.name: White-coat effect
categories.clinical_meaning: BP elevated only in clinical setting, normal at home
categories.action: Avoid unnecessary medication intensification, confirm with home readings

category.description: Controlled hypertension / Uncontrolled hypertension / Masked uncontrolled hypertension / White-coat effect

officeHigh.description: true if office BP exceeds threshold

outOfOfficeHigh.description: true if out-of-office BP exceeds threshold

requiresMedicationChange.description: true for uncontrolled or masked uncontrolled

thresholds.sbp: N/A - classification for chronic management, not acute crisis

escalationLogic.ifAnySymptomPresent: N/A - separate hypertensive crisis protocol applies

nextStepRouting.providerSuggestion: Controlled: Continue current regimen, follow up in 3-6 months; Uncontrolled/Masked: Intensify therapy, follow up in 4 weeks; White-coat: Confirm with ambulatory monitoring, avoid medication changes
nextStepRouting.followUpTiming: Controlled: 3-6 months; Uncontrolled/Masked: 4 weeks; White-coat: 6-12 months with home monitoring confirmation
nextStepRouting.repeatMeasurement: Controlled: Routine visits; Uncontrolled/Masked: At each medication adjustment; White-coat: Repeat home monitoring periodically
nextStepRouting.navigatorEscalation: Flag uncontrolled and masked uncontrolled for care team review and medication adjustment
nextStepRouting.documentation: Log BP category to hypertension registry, update medication effectiveness record
